evals.prompt: I need to protect all routes under /dashboard in my Astro app. If the user doesn't have a session_token cookie, redirect them to /login. If they do have a token, let them through.
evals.expected_output: Middleware with defineMiddleware, checks pathname.startsWith('/dashboard'), reads session_token cookie, redirects to /login if missing, calls next() if present

evals.prompt: My middleware needs to do three things: 1) log all requests with timing, 2) check authentication for /app/* routes, 3) check admin role for /admin/* routes. How do I organize multiple validations in Astro middleware?
evals.expected_output: Either sequential checks in one middleware OR use sequence() to compose multiple defineMiddleware functions. Shows logging middleware, auth middleware, and rbac middleware composed together

evals.prompt: I want to pass the authenticated user's data from middleware to my pages. How do I set up context.locals properly with TypeScript types so I can access user.id and user.email in my Astro pages?
evals.expected_output: Middleware sets context.locals.user = {...}, env.d.ts declares App.Locals interface with user property typed, page accesses Astro.locals.user with full type support

evals.prompt: I have JWT tokens stored in cookies. In my middleware, I want to decode the JWT to get user info and pass it to pages, but I don't want to validate the signature in middleware - my backend API will do that. How should I structure this?
evals.expected_output: Middleware reads cookie, decodes JWT payload without verification, extracts user data, sets context.locals.token and context.locals.user, explains that backend validates on actual API calls
